reason: Gattaca’s near-future genetic ethics and thriller pacing explore human modification morality.
Strange Days probes tech’s dark side and memory ethics in a gritty, near-future thriller.
Ghost in the Shell tackles AI identity and ethics in a grounded cyberpunk thriller.
Sphere questions human alteration and perception in an isolated near-future sci-fi setting.
Twelve Monkeys examines viral tech failure and time’s ethics in a tense, grounded thriller.
Contact explores the morality of science and technology in a grounded, emotional near-future story.
The Net delves into digital identity and surveillance ethics in a taut techno-thriller.
Disclosure tackles sexual and tech power dynamics in a corporate thriller context.
Sneakers explores surveillance and cryptography ethics with smart, grounded intrigue.
Terminator 2 questions AI’s role and human survival in a near-future, action-thriller setting.
